description: Create all missing delivery-related mobile API routes that the mobile app calls but currently get 404: /delivery/dashboard, /delivery/orders/[id], /delivery/collect, /delivery/complete, /delivery/acknowledge, /delivery/location, /delivery/orders/[id]/collection, /delivery/orders/[id]/complete, /delivery/orders/[id]/damage, /delivery/orders/[id]/pickup, /delivery/assignments/pending
steps: Create apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/delivery/dashboard/route.ts - GET endpoint returning delivery partner's assigned orders with earnings summary. Use authenticateMobileRequest, check role is DELIVERY_PARTNER. Query orders assigned to the user with status in [READY_FOR_DELIVERY, OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, ARRIVING]. Return {orders: [{id, orderNumber, customerName, address, total, status, itemCount, createdAt}], deliveriesToday, cashCollected, upiCollected}. Follow the DeliveryDashboardData type from packages/shared.

Create apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/delivery/orders/[id]/route.ts - GET endpoint returning full order detail for delivery partner. Use authenticateMobileRequest, check role DELIVERY_PARTNER and order.deliveryPartnerId === auth.userId. Return full order detail matching OrderDetail type: items, customer info, address, payment info, status.

Create apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/delivery/collect/route.ts - POST endpoint to record payment collection. Accept {orderId, cashCollected, upiCollected, upiReference?}. Reuse the business logic pattern from apps/web/app/api/delivery/collect/route.ts but use authenticateMobileRequest instead of authenticateDeliveryPartnerRequest. Use prisma directly with the same collectionBalance logic (import from @/lib/delivery-money).

Create apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/delivery/complete/route.ts - POST endpoint to mark delivery complete with OTP. Accept {orderId, otp}. Reuse the business logic pattern from apps/web/app/api/delivery/complete/route.ts but use authenticateMobileRequest.

Create apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/delivery/acknowledge/route.ts - POST endpoint to acknowledge a delivery assignment. Accept {eventId, orderId}. Update the AssignmentEvent with acknowledgedAt and update order deliveryAlertAckAt. Follow pattern from apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/assignments/route.ts POST handler.

Create apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/delivery/location/route.ts - POST endpoint to update delivery partner GPS. Accept {latitude, longitude, heading?}. Reuse logic from apps/web/app/api/delivery/location/route.ts but with authenticateMobileRequest.

Create apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/delivery/orders/[id]/collection/route.ts - GET returns collection status for the order, POST records a collection. For GET: query DeliveryCollection for the orderId. For POST: accept {amount, method} and create/update collection.

Create apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/delivery/orders/[id]/complete/route.ts - POST endpoint to mark specific order as delivered (simpler variant without OTP for the /orders/[id]/complete pattern used in app/orders/[id]/complete.tsx). Update order status to DELIVERED, create OrderEvent.

Create apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/delivery/orders/[id]/damage/route.ts - POST endpoint to report damage. Accept {items: [{productId, name, quantity, reason}]}. Create DeliveryAdjustment records. Follow pattern from apps/web/app/api/delivery/damage/route.ts but simplified for mobile.

Create apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/delivery/orders/[id]/pickup/route.ts - POST endpoint to confirm pickup from store. Update order status from READY_FOR_DELIVERY to OUT_FOR_DELIVERY, create OrderEvent.

Create apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/delivery/assignments/pending/route.ts - GET endpoint returning pending (unacknowledged) assignment events for the current delivery partner. Query AssignmentEvent where partnerId = auth.userId and acknowledgedAt is null.
acceptance_criteria: All 11 route files exist under apps/web/app/api/mobile/v1/delivery/
Each route uses authenticateMobileRequest from @/lib/mobile-auth for authentication
Each route validates the user role is DELIVERY_PARTNER
TypeScript compiles without errors (npx tsc --noEmit in apps/web)
The response shapes match what the mobile app expects based on how it uses the data
